edges.description: Intracellular replication produces the acute febrile, pneumonic, and hepatic syndromes.
edges.source: Inhalation and Intracellular Replication in Alveolar Macrophages

edges.description: The organism's ribosome is the molecular target of tetracycline therapy.
edges.source: Inhalation and Intracellular Replication in Alveolar Macrophages

edges.target: Inhalation and Intracellular Replication in Alveolar Macrophages

nodes.description: Acute Q fever manifests as one of three syndromes: a nonspecific febrile illness, pneumonia (which can range from mild to severe requiring ventilation, classically with multiple round opacities on chest radiography), or hepatitis.

evidence.snippet: There are three distinct clinical syndromes of the acute form of the illness: nonspecific febrile illness, pneumonia, and hepatitis.

nodes.description: C. burnetii depends on its bacterial ribosome for protein synthesis. Doxycycline, a tetracycline, binds the 30S ribosomal subunit and arrests bacterial protein synthesis; this ribosomal target — combined with doxycycline's intracellular penetration — is why a tetracycline rather than a beta-lactam is first-line.

evidence.snippet: The ribosome is one of the main antibiotic targets in the bacterial cell.

nodes.description: Tetracycline antibiotic that accumulates intracellularly and inhibits bacterial protein synthesis at the 30S ribosome; the preferred treatment for Q fever because it reaches the intracellular organism. A fluoroquinolone is an alternative; macrolide susceptibility is variable.

evidence.snippet: Treatment with doxycycline or a fluoroquinolone is preferred.

nodes.description: Q fever follows inhalation of aerosolized Coxiella burnetii, which is an obligate intracellular bacterium that replicates within alveolar macrophages in a lysosome-derived replicative vacuole. This intracellular lifestyle is the basis for the requirement for cell-penetrant antibiotics, because beta-lactams cannot reach the cytoplasmic organism.
nodes.id: Inhalation and Intracellular Replication in Alveolar Macrophages

biological_processes: biological process involved in interaction with host

evidence.snippet: C. burnetii (Cb) is an obligate intracellular bacterial pathogen that replicates within alveolar macrophages following aerosol infection.
This spore-forming microorganism is a small gram-negative coccobacillus that is an obligate intracellular parasite.
